Input values are plausible and consistent with USDA component data for this dish type. Confidence retained at medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on stuffed pepper with mixed meat and vegetable filling
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chiles en Nogada. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous stuffed vegetable dishes with mixed meat and fruit fillings from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish is low in carbohydrates (6.67 g/100g), high in protein and fat which blunt glycaemic response, and contains dietary fibre (1.78 g/100g). Glycemic load per 450 g serving = (35 × 6.67 × 4.5) / 100 ≈ 10.5 g, classified as medium GL. Estimation is conservative and defensible.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 177.78 mg/100g; potassium-rich ingredients (poblano pepper, tomato, fruit) support vasodilation
hypertension.guidance: Chiles en Nogada is low in sodium at 177.78 mg/100g and the potassium-rich vegetable and fruit components support blood pressure regulation; individuals with hypertension should avoid adding extra salt during preparation and moderate the walnut cream sauce portion due to its saturated fat content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content (6.67 g/100g), moderate protein (7.78 g/100g), dietary fibre (1.78 g/100g), and healthy fats from walnuts moderate postprandial glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 6.67 g carbohydrate per 100 g, a low estimated GI of 35, and a glycemic load of approximately 10.5 per standard serving, Chiles en Nogada is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in a single-pepper portion; the natural fruit sugars in the picadillo filling should be monitored and refined carbohydrate accompaniments avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.44 g/100g and cholesterol 26.67 mg/100g from minced beef, pork, cream cheese, and goat cheese; partially offset by walnut omega-3 ALA and pomegranate antioxidants
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content (4.44 g/100g) and cholesterol (26.67 mg/100g) from the red meat picadillo and dairy-based nogada sauce represent a moderate cardiovascular concern; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to one pepper, consider substituting minced beef and pork with lean poultry, and use reduced-fat cheese alternatives in the walnut sauce.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Minced beef and pork are primary protein sources; both are rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), a precursor to pro-inflammatory eicosanoids
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Chiles en Nogada in its traditional form is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the red meat picadillo filling; substituting minced beef and pork with ground turkey or chicken significantly reduces arachidonic acid load, and the walnuts in the nogada sauce provide beneficial omega-3 ALA if the meat substitution is made.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chiles en Nogada is a celebrated Mexican dish originating from the state of Puebla, traditionally prepared to honour Mexican Independence Day in September. The dish consists of a whole poblano pepper that is roasted and peeled, then stuffed with a savoury-sweet picadillo filling made from minced beef and pork cooked with diced apple, pear, peach, raisins, almonds, tomato, onion, garlic, and warming spices including cinnamon and cloves. The stuffed pepper is then covered with a cold, creamy walnut sauce (nogada) made from fresh walnuts, cream cheese, goat cheese, sherry, and milk, and garnished with ruby-red pomegranate seeds and fresh flat-leaf parsley — the green, white, and red colours representing the Mexican flag. The flavour profile is complex: the pepper provides mild heat and slight bitterness, the picadillo is savoury with sweet fruit notes, and the nogada is rich, creamy, and subtly nutty. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 167 kcal, 7.8 g protein, 12.2 g fat (4.4 g saturated), 6.7 g carbohydrate, 3.3 g sugar, 1.8 g dietary fibre, and 178 mg sodium. The low carbohydrate content and moderate protein make it suitable for low-glycaemic dietary patterns. The red meat content raises concerns for rheumatoid arthritis and cardiovascular disease management. Walnuts contribute omega-3 ALA and pomegranate seeds provide polyphenol antioxidants. A standard serving is one stuffed pepper with sauce, approximately 450 g.

Nutrient values are derived from the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central component entries for stuffed pepper with meat, ground beef, ground pork, walnuts, cream cheese, and poblano pepper. Values are internally consistent and plausible for this dish type. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework. Confidence is medium.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central as a single entry; component-level USDA data for walnuts and ground beef would be required for a full fatty acid profile, which is beyond the scope of this estimation. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for the composite dish is not reported in the input record and no single USDA entry covers this exact preparation; estimated ~280 mg/100g was cited in the input health suitability notes but is not confirmed by a named approved source entry and has therefore been set to -1 pending verification. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite traditional Mexican dish are not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved source consulted as of 2026-04-16. Input ethnic_tags contained 'Taco', 'Burrito', and 'Naan' which are incorrect for this dish and have been removed. Input country_of_origin 'Singapore' and region 'Southeast Asia' are incorrect for this Mexican dish and have been corrected to Mexico (Puebla) and Latin America respectively. Gluten-free dietary flag retained from input; note that cross-contamination risk exists depending on preparation environment. Trans fat 0.11 g/100g is a low but non-zero value consistent with small amounts of naturally occurring trans fats in ruminant-derived meat and dairy components.
